title: One Scene To Sell Them All
display_title: One Scene To Sell Them All
wikitext:
[]: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Jan 06, 2017 9:45 pm If you were pitching Evangelion to a friend who had no knowledge of the property, what scene would you choose to show them to give them a taste of what the series has to offer?

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Director Black » Fri Jan 06, 2017 11:56 pm For action: - Unit 01 breaking out of Leliel - Units 00 and 01 fighting Ramiel For psychological - Misato surviving second impact - The opening scene of episode 21 Or I can show the person these two AMVs for a taste of what's in it.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 07, 2017 4:56 am The same scene that I saw first... Rei's self destruct of 00 to stop Armisarael.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Sat Jan 07, 2017 7:08 am NemZ wrote: The same scene that I saw first... Rei's self destruct of 00 to stop Armisarael.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jan 07, 2017 7:12 am Sicarius VI wrote: How'd you manage to see that first?

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Sat Jan 07, 2017 7:24 am ^I feel like that must've been very confusing when you first watched it too. On top of intriguing and wacky. I think the way I first saw Eva was through Glass Reflections/Arkada's review of it. Actually considering his reviews are spoiler free and he actually highly recommends the show it wasn't a bad way to be introduced. I say the Geofront part because I remember first time watching episode 1 that part for whatever reason got me hooked like "Ok, I need to see the rest of this."

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 07, 2017 8:01 am Sicarius VI wrote: How'd you manage to see that first?

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 07, 2017 8:05 am Allow me to be out of touch with everyone. Selecting one scene out of an action film as a selling point usually comes out of the idea that you can sell the audience on our spectacle alone. This is usually works with live-action films, as the sense of spectacle tends to be more effective greater in that medium than it is with animation, and it also tends to be executed in late night talk show settings. ("Joseph Gordon-Levett brought us a clip from that Inception movie he was in, let's watch." *hallway fight scene plays*) This has been done in other venues, though. There was a theatrical run of the first 10 minutes of Disney's Dinosaur that screened before other movies in order to gain the attention from audiences, again, with the sheer spectacle of the live-action aesthetic placed on the dinosaur characters in that part of the movie. And the airplane hijacking scene from the opening of The Dark Knight Rises had a good run on the internet before the movie was finally released to theaters. But again, this is mainly done for live-action films, and usually done to showcase spectacle. It's difficult to showcase spectacle for animated films unless the animation is either really trippy, or simply stop-motion animated. (General audiences still get more of kick out of seeing stop-motion animation, as the amount of hard work that goes into that is more tangible to them, as opposed to the concept of clicking away on a computer screen in order to create Toy Story 3.) So, there really wouldn't be any good single scenes or moments I could use in order to persuade someone to watch Evangelion. Trailers and TV spots tend to work best for that, but Japanese trailers don't work towards the expectations that Western audiences have for them, and Western-edited trailers tend to be plagued with distractingly bad English dubs that don't necessarily reflect the intentions of the original filmmakers. So I would probably just schedule a viewing with them, and let their interest in the series grow from that first viewing. I've done this before, and it's worked out pretty well for me.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Geometer » Sat Jan 07, 2017 9:38 am Asuka's fight with the MP eva's if I wanted to hook them, although that would set the bar too high.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 07, 2017 6:08 pm When I came to the series, my knowledge of it was limited to the fact that the ending was somehow controversial, and that it came with the recommendation that if you were to watch one giant robot anime, you should make it this one -- so I didn't need to be sold any further to start watching it. I'm not sure that seeing any out-of-context material would have made a positive impact, even the scene which made my ears prick up when I reached it, the one with Gendo and the Committee in episode 2, when the ulterior motives behind the goings-on was first hinted at.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Mon Jan 09, 2017 1:33 am I use Komm Susser Tod as a song divorced from any actual context. The accompanying visuals do make it the most memorable scene, but only when you understand the context.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by sephirotic » Mon Jan 09, 2017 2:03 am Definetely Unit 01 escaping from leliel, but showing Shinji depressed inside it First for the psychological build up. Tough is a real shame how that is exactly the only Episode with awful video quality. Ep 16 was the first i saw entirely after glimpsing the second half of episode 15 and thinking Eva was an animated soap opera. The shock was imense.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by The Cruel » Tue Jan 10, 2017 9:05 pm The first fight with Sachiel is the introduction into the bloodshed that is the war against the angels and Eva overall.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Director Black » Tue Jan 10, 2017 10:19 pm Thinking about it more, I wouldn't use any scene. Eva is best when you're giving little to no details (And tell them how it helped me with my depression, but that's another story).

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 11, 2017 5:31 am If this isn't the kind of person who can be compelled to watch something with intriguing words alone, just play them Masayuki's Cruel Angel's Thesis music video. If that doesn't work, nothing will.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Director Black » Wed Jan 11, 2017 7:35 pm Reichu wrote: If this isn't the kind of person who can be compelled to watch something with intriguing words alone, just play them Masayuki's Cruel Angel's Thesis music video. If that doesn't work, nothing will.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Jan 17, 2017 8:49 am Director Black wrote: Or Evangelion Opus (The AMV with Bohemian Rhapsody).

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Jan 17, 2017 9:28 am I tried with the "I'm so fucked up" scene but it didn't work. Don't try it.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Director Black » Tue Jan 17, 2017 9:08 pm Joseki wrote: I tried with the "I'm so fucked up" scene but it didn't work. Don't try it.

[]: Re: "One Scene To Sell Them All"             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jan 18, 2017 1:03 pm Not a scene, but Masayuki's video for Tamashii no Rufuran (Soul's Refrain). Nothing else I've tried gets people's attention quite like that video does.
